findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier notification priority system requires changes to the existing notification system architecture to support priority levels. This includes updating the database schema and modifying the notification delivery logic.
assumptions: Existing notification system can be modified to support new priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Assess current database schema for necessary changes.

findings.title: Android 14 Notification Delivery Failure
findings.description: Push notifications are failing on Android 14 devices, which affects a significant portion of the user base. This issue needs to be resolved to ensure reliable notification delivery.
assumptions: The issue is solely related to Android 14 and not a broader system failure.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the delivery callback issue on Android 14.

findings.title: ML-Based Notification Batching Implementation
findings.description: Implementing ML-based notification batching requires the development of a machine learning model to analyze user engagement patterns. This will involve data collection, model training, and integration into the notification system.
assumptions: Sufficient user engagement data is available for model training.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Evaluate existing ML frameworks for feasibility.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will necessitate coordination between the backend and frontend teams to ensure proper integration.
assumptions: The existing API can be extended to support new endpoints.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Plan for API development and frontend integration.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency Reduction
findings.description: Reducing notification delivery latency requires significant architectural changes, including moving to asynchronous database writes and implementing batch inserts. This poses a risk of introducing new issues during implementation.
assumptions: Current system can handle asynchronous operations without major refactoring.

evidence.excerpt: Root cause appears to be synchronous database writes in the delivery pipeline.

recommendations: Conduct thorough testing during the transition to async writes.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the implementation of the SmartNotify system. Key components such as notification priority levels and delivery latency reduction are flagged as high-impact areas requiring immediate attention, while ML-based batching and user preferences are planned for later phases.
